messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a VideoProject aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve using discriminated unions for Command, Event, and State. Reject reopening an archived project with a typed DomainError.

Write an event-sourced Decider for an EditSession aggregate. Constraints: strict types, no any, exhaustive switch with never default, typed DomainError, reject committing an EditSession that has no pending cuts.

Explain briefly how a Decider captures the lifecycle of a RenderJob aggregate, then show a typed implementation with queue, start, progress, finish, and fail commands.

A Decider bundles pure decide/evolve/initialState for one aggregate. decide(command, state) returns events or throws a typed domain error. evolve(state, event) folds events back into state exhaustively. No I/O lives in either function.
Build a pure Decider for a ColorGrade aggregate with commands create, applyLut, approve, revoke. Model state as a discriminated union. Reject revoking an ungraded asset.

Build a pure Decider for a SoundtrackCue aggregate. Commands: schedule, mute, unmute, finalize. Model state with discriminated union. Use exhaustive switch with never default. Reject finalizing a muted cue.
Implement a SceneShot Decider in TypeScript with strict types, no any, all commands and events typed via discriminated union, exhaustive never-default branches.
What's the v5 way to project a Transcript read model from domain events? Show a minimal project(view, event): View that folds captioning events into a flat, query-friendly view.
Build a projection that folds CutList events into a summary view. The view should track totalCuts, totalDurationMs, and the last editor. Ignore events that don't belong to the view.
Write a StoryboardFrame read-model projection. View columns: id, panelCount, firstSketchAt, approvedAt, retiredAt. Skip unrelated events.

Explain briefly how a read model differs from an aggregate evolve, then write a VFXShot projection that tracks renders, lastCompositedAt, and a flag for whether review is cleared.

An aggregate evolve reconstructs authoritative state used for decide; a projection denormalises events into a read view that serves queries. Projections can lag and can be rebuilt; they do NOT drive invariants.
What's the v5 way to load and upgrade a Snapshot<VideoProject>? Write a Snapshot<S> type, CURRENT_VERSION const, two upgraders (v1 to v2, v2 to v3), and a load function that walks upgraders in order.

Build a Snapshot<RenderJob> loader with versioned upgrades from v1 through v3. The v1 to v2 upgrade splits a combined 'status' string into discriminated state; v2 to v3 adds 'percent'. Include a CURRENT_VERSION const and an upgradeSnapshot function.
Build an appendToStream wrapper for EditSession that enforces optimistic concurrency. Read revision, decide, append with expectedRevision. On mismatch surface ConcurrencyConflict.
How do I write RenderJob commands with optimistic concurrency using Effect.gen? Include a typed ConcurrencyConflict error raised on revision mismatch.
Build a CommandHandler for ColorGrade that loads the stream, runs decide, and appends. Use a local EventStore interface; no external imports.
Write a SoundtrackCue CommandHandler in Effect.gen that loads, decides, and appends. Keep the EventStore interface local.
How do I build a typed SceneShot CommandHandler that orchestrates readStream, decide, and appendToStream? Keep domain logic out of the handler.
Build a Transcript CommandHandler. Commands: start, addSegment, publish. Use a local EventStore interface and route the command through decide.
Write a generic aggregateStream helper that folds an async iterable of EditSession events into state, then show a when reducer using it.
Write a StoryboardFrame when reducer and show aggregateStream folding a stored event list back into current state.
How do I use a typed aggregateStream to rebuild a VFXShot aggregate state from stored events? Include a when function with exhaustive never default.

Explain briefly how a pure when reducer differs from an imperative handler, then implement a DailyRush when that folds rush events into state.

A pure when reducer is (state, event) => state, total, deterministic, and side-effect free. It is the fold used to rebuild aggregate state. An imperative handler mutates external systems (DB, queues) and consumes commands, not events. when is what you call from aggregateStream; handlers are what you call from the command side.
Build a Transcript read-model rebuild function. It reads from a given fromRevision in batchSize chunks, calls project per event, persists the batch, and advances the cursor.
How do I rebuild a CutList read model from revision 0 with resumable chunked progress? Show a rebuild function keyed on a from cursor so a crash can resume.
Implement a minimal CQRS boundary for VideoProject. handleCommand uses the Decider + EventStore. queryView reads from a read store. The two sides share events, not state.
Write a CQRS boundary for RenderJob. Writes flow through decide + appendToStream. Reads flow through a RenderJobQueueView read store. Share events, not state.
Build an appendWithOutbox transaction for RenderJob. Insert outbox rows and append to the event stream in one unit of work. A separate poller (commented, not implemented) publishes the rows.
Add a combine helper that merges two Deciders into one for a VideoProject coordination aggregate. Pattern match on a top-level command type to route to sub-decider. Keep strict types.

Add a buildSnapshot policy to this RenderJob Decider that emits a Snapshot every N events. Return null when no snapshot should be built.

```ts
export const renderJobDecider = { initialState, decide, evolve };
```
Write a minimal VideoProject integration translator plus an appendToOutbox function that persists the produced rows alongside the event append. Include both; make translate pure.
